tr.td: Periodic comprehensive preventive medicine reevaluation and management of an individual including an age and gender appropriate history, examination, counseling/anticipatory guidance/risk factor reduction interventions, and the ordering of laboratory/diagnostic procedures, established patient; 65 years and older

Office or other outpatient visit for the evaluation and management of an established patient, which requires at least 2 of these 3 key components: A problem focused history; A problem focused examination; Straightforward medical decision making. Counseling and/or coordination of care with other physicians, other qualified health care professionals, or agencies are provided consistent with the nature of the problem(s) and the patient's and/or family's needs. Usually, the presenting problem(s) are self limited or minor. Typically, 10 minutes are spent face-to-face with the patient and/or family.
Collection of venous blood by venipuncture
Lipid Panel with Calc LDL This panel must include the following: Lipid (83921)

Urinalysis, by dip stick or tablet reagent for bilirubin, glucose, hemoglobin, ketones, leukocytes, nitrite, pH, protein, specific gravity, urobilinogen, any number of these constituents; automated, without microscopy

Urinalysis, by dip stick or tablet reagent for bilirubin, glucose, hemoglobin, ketones, leukocytes, nitrite, pH, protein, specific gravity, urobilinogen, any number of these constituents; automated, without microscopy

Urinalysis, by dip stick or tablet reagent for bilirubin, glucose, hemoglobin, ketones, leukocytes, nitrite, pH, protein, specific gravity, urobilinogen, any number of these constituents; automated, without microscopy

Office or other outpatient visit for the evaluation and management of an established patient, which requires at least 2 of these 3 key components: A detailed history; A detailed examination; Medical decision making of moderate complexity. Counseling and/or coordination of care with other physicians, other qualified health care professionals, or agencies are provided consistent with the nature of the problem(s) and the patient's and/or family's needs. Usually, the presenting problem(s) are of moderate to high severity. Typically, 25 minutes are spent face-to-face with the patient and/or family.

Office or other outpatient visit for the evaluation and management of an established patient, which requires at least 2 of these 3 key components: A problem focused history; A problem focused examination; Straightforward medical decision making. Counseling and/or coordination of care with other physicians, other qualified health care professionals, or agencies are provided consistent with the nature of the problem(s) and the patient's and/or family's needs. Usually, the presenting problem(s) are self limited or minor. Typically, 10 minutes are spent face-to-face with the patient and/or family.

Office or other outpatient visit for the evaluation and management of an established patient, which requires at least 2 of these 3 key components: A detailed history; A detailed examination; Medical decision making of moderate complexity. Counseling and/or coordination of care with other physicians, other qualified health care professionals, or agencies are provided consistent with the nature of the problem(s) and the patient's and/or family's needs. Usually, the presenting problem(s) are of moderate to high severity. Typically, 25 minutes are spent face-to-face with the patient and/or family.

Full Progress Note signed by Patricia Lavinus, NP/G. Lockard, MD
Chief Complaint: 27 year old male: Establishing PCP and Discuss Medication

History of Present Illness: Test Patient is a 27 year old male present in office for establishing a new provider due to Ambrish Patel PA-C no longer working for Family Practice Associates and discuss medication options for his GI issues.

Review of Systems: - General: denies fever, chills, fatigue - Eyes: denies visual disturbances - ENT: no tooth pain, denies ear pain - CV: denies chest pain, LE edema, palpitations - Pulm: denies dyspnea, cough - GI: denies diarrhea, consitpation, nausea, vomiting - Endo: denies excessive thirst - Neuro: denies headache, weakness, numbness/tingling of extremities, no motor or sensory deficits - MSK: denies joint pain or swelling - Skin: no rashes or ulcers - Psych: denies SI and HI. No depression and anxiety symptoms at the moment. Seeing Psych and doing well.
Past Medical History: -Bipolar type 2 -Anxiety -Depression
Family History: ======== Structured Family History ======== Patient reports their family members have no significant health history

Social History: - Employment: Construction - Marital Status: Married - Tobacco: Current every day smoker - Pipe Smoker: No - Cigar Smoker: No - Chewing Tobacco User: No - Electronic Cigarette User: No - Alcohol: No - Illicit Drug Use: No
Allergies: No Known Allergies

Medications: 1) Abilify 5 mg tablet, Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily 2) buspirone 10 mg tablet, Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily 3) Nexium 40 mg capsule,delayed release, Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily 4) One Daily Multivitamin tablet, Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily 5) Prozac 20 mg capsule, Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily 6) Topamax 25 mg tablet, Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily 7) trazodone 50 mg tablet, Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily

Physical Examination: - General: well groomed, in NAD sitting up on exam table - HEENT: head atraumatic, bilateral TM pearly grey with good light reflex, no drainage in external canal. Nares patent. Pharynx pink, without tonsillar enlargement or exudates. No carotid bruits, thyroid size normal without masses or nodules, trachea midline - CV: Regular rate, regular rhythm, no murmurs, no pedal edema. 2+ radial pulses - Pulm: thorax symmetric with good excursion, CTAB - GI: non tender all 4 quadrants, no organomegaly, normal bowel sounds - Skin: warm, dry and intact without rashes - Neuro: alert, awake - MSK: normal gait and station - Psych: appropriate affect, conversant, normal insight and judgement. Patients wife is also very supportive.

Assessment Plan: # Bipolar II disorder (F31.81): Controlled with Abilify. Psychiatrist manages medication. # Mixed anxiety and depressive disorder (F41.8): Controlled with Buspirone and Prozac. Psychiatrist manages medications. # Obstructive sleep apnea (G47.33): Sleep Study with Dr.Puryear ordered. # Mixed insomnia (G47.09): Controlled with Trazadone. # Gastric reflux (K21.9): Prescribed Nexium. Follow up in one month to see how medication is working. # Body mass index [BMI] 19.9 or less, adult (Z68.1): Normal range BMI
Follow up in one month to review how Nexium medication is working. If Nexium does not work we will refer Carmen to Gastro Dr.Eid office. Sleep Study with Dr.Puryear office has been ordered and will contact him to set up an appointment.

Full Progress Note signed by Phyllis Waggoner, NP/R. Kaundal, MD
Chief Complaint: Annual Wellness Visit

History of Present Illness: Patient Test, male, 121 years of age is here for his Annual Medicare Wellness Visit to include his Health Risk Assessment
Past Medical History;
Pneumovax (rec once > 65): Tdap (rec every 10 years): Influenza (rec yearly): Mammogram (rec yearly female): PSA (rec yearly males) {no longer covered}: DEXA (rec every 2 years): Colon CA screen: Advanced Directive on file? Y/N Tobacco Use (rec no smoking): Y/N (smoking increases risks of death, MI, CVA, and CA) A1C: PHQ-9:
1. During the past four weeks, how much have you been bothered by emotional problems such as feeling anxious, depressed, irritable, sad, or downhearted and blue? Not at all () Slightly () Moderately () Quite a bit () Extremely ()
2. During the past four weeks, has your physical and emotional healkth limited your social activities with family, friends, neighbors, or groups? Not at all () Slightly () Moderately () Quite a bit () Extremely ()
3. During the past four weeks, how much bodily pain have you generally had? Not at all () Slightly () Moderately () Quite a bit () Extremely ()
4. During the past four weeks, was someone available to help you if you needed and wanted help? (For example, if you felt very nervous, lonely, or blue; got sick and had to stay in bed; needed someone to talk to; needed help with daily chores; or needed help just taking care of yourself.) Not at all () Slightly () Moderately () Quite a bit () Extremely ()
5. During the past four weeks, what was the hardest physical activity you could do for at least two minutes? Very heavy () Heavy () Moderate () Light () Very Light ()
6. Can you get to places out of walking distance without help? For example, can you travel alone or drive our own car? Y/N
7. Can you go shopping for groceries or clothes without someone's help? Y/N
8. Can you prepare your own meals? Y/N
9. Can you do your housework without help Y/N
10. Because of any health problems, do you need the help of another person with your personal care needs such as eating, bathing, dressing, or getting around the house? Y/N
11. Can you handle your own money without help? Y/N
12. During the past four weeks, how would you rate your health in general? Excellent () Very Good () Good () Fair () Poor ()
13. How have things been going for you during the past four weeks? Pretty well () Good and bad parts about equal () Pretty bad () Very bad ()
